findings.description: The HTML renders the title elements (FUNDING AUTHORIZATION, SFY 2016-2017, AUTHORIZATION #1) as separate sections with a horizontal rule, whereas the PDF groups them as a single centered block. The overall structure is preserved but the visual grouping differs slightly.
findings.evidence: PDF: Three lines centered together as a unified title block. HTML: Title split with a horizontal rule separator between 'FUNDING AUTHORIZATION' and 'SFY 2016-2017'.

findings.description: The signature area and date layout differs. In the PDF, the signature and date are on the same line with the date right-aligned. In the HTML, the date appears inline after the signature line.
findings.evidence: PDF: Signature line on left, 'August 31, 2016' and 'Date' right-aligned. HTML: Date appears after the signature line text in a more linear flow.

findings.description: The 'County: __RICHMOND__' line uses bold formatting in the HTML for 'County:' and 'RICHMOND' which matches the PDF, but the underline fill after RICHMOND is shorter in the HTML.
The signature image/handwriting appears identical in both versions and is correctly placed above the Division of Aging & Adult Services line.
findings.evidence: Both versions show the same cursive signature 'Suzanne P. Merrill'.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy is broken: h1 → h2 → h3 used for what is essentially a single multi-line title block. 'SFY 2016-2017' and 'AUTHORIZATION #1' are not subsections but part of the same title. More importantly, heading levels skip context — the document doesn't have true subsections warranting h2 and h3.
findings.evidence:
HTML uses

FUNDING AUTHORIZATION

,

SFY 2016- 2017

,

AUTHORIZATION #1

. In the PDF these are all part of the same centered title block, not a hierarchical structure.

findings.description: The HTML contains nested duplicate <main> tags, which while not content duplication per se, is a structural error that could cause rendering issues.

Alt text for the signature image identifies the signer as 'Suzanne P. Merrill'. While this appears to be a reasonable reading of the handwritten signature, verification from the PDF alone is uncertain since the name is only present as a signature image.
findings.evidence: alt="Signature of Suzanne P. Merrill" — the PDF shows a handwritten signature but no typed name to confirm.
